title: Enable release-preparation mode for manually dispatched Smoke

sources.reason: Manual strict Smoke currently runs release distribution without candidate preparation mode.

problemStatement: The release workflow dispatches Smoke before creating the new tag, but manually dispatched Smoke sets AI_RELEASE_PREPARATION=0 and rejects the approved unreleased v0.5.30 candidate against public v0.5.29.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: workflow_dispatch Smoke enables release-preparation mode while pull-request and main-push behavior remains enabled.
Strict Smoke still runs all quality, installer, AI, and source-bound checks.

riskAssessment.reason: The change affects only the preparation-mode selector; all Smoke checks remain blocking.

executionDecision.reason: The failure is reproduced and the minimal selector correction is identified.

preReviewWarnings: Do not weaken strict Smoke or source identity checks.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record the observed workflow failure and regression evidence.

acceptance: Manual Smoke enables AI_RELEASE_PREPARATION=1 for prepublication release candidates.
Pull request and main-push preparation behavior remains intact.
Full quality and workflow regression checks pass.
guidelines: Make the smallest workflow change supported by the observed failure.

intent.problem: The prepublication Smoke dispatch rejects the next approved release because it does not know it is a candidate.
constraints: Do not change version or release identity.
intent.rationale: Manual Smoke is the release workflow's prepublication verification path and must use the same candidate semantics as PR/main-push Smoke.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized the complete release cycle.

rollbackNote: Revert the preparation-mode expression and regression evidence if manual Smoke semantics remain incorrect.
